subject: Biology
title: The Carbon Cycle
description: A short lesson to help you apply The Carbon Cycle.

hook.text: Carbon atoms cycle between air, water, rock, and living things in a loop that never stops.

buildup.text: Plants pull CO₂ from the air during photosynthesis and lock carbon into sugars.

discovery.text: Animals eat plants and release carbon back as CO₂ through respiration.

twist.text: Fossil fuels are ancient carbon locked underground. Burning them floods the atmosphere with CO₂.

climax.text: Excess atmospheric carbon drives global warming, disrupting ecosystems worldwide.

punchline.text: Carbon cycles naturally. We sped it up.

quiz.question: How do fossil fuels relate to the carbon cycle?
options: Burning them releases ancient carbon into the atmosphere
They absorb carbon from the air
They have no effect on carbon levels
